segments.title: Tracking battlecruiser costs from Invincible to Hood
segments.summary: A brief answer explains why the rising cost of British battlecruisers between HMS Invincible and HMS Hood is difficult to chart precisely.
segments.body: Dr. Clarke says the cost trend can be sketched as a rising curve, but the figures for every intervening ship are not all known. The moment highlights how gaps in comparable procurement data complicate attempts to measure the pace of warship cost growth.
segments.question: Can the cost growth of British battlecruisers between HMS Invincible and HMS Hood be tracked?
segments.answerShort: Only roughly: the trend rises, but incomplete cost figures for the ships between Invincible and Hood prevent a precise chart.

evidence.note: Dr. Clarke says the intervening ship costs are not all known, although they can be represented approximately as a rising curve.
